subject: John Carmack
evidence_standard: L1 = verbatim from primary sources (.plan files, interviews, keynotes), L2 = reported/documented, L3 = inferred

biography.education: University of Missouri–Kansas City (two semesters, withdrew to pursue programming)
biography.notable_early_incident: As a teenager, broke into a school to steal Apple II computers; served one year in a juvenile home. Later attributed the experience to clarifying his focus on legitimate paths.
biography.personal: Married Katherine Kang 2000, divorced 2021. Two sons. Currently partnered with Trista DeLeon (2022). Received Domino's Pizza daily at 1995 prices for 15+ years during id Software era. Gave away Ferrari 328 as QuakeCon tournament prize (1997). Founded and self-funded Armadillo Aerospace (~$1M/year). Black belt equivalents in Brazilian jiu-jitsu and Judo. Libertarian. Atheist.

key_facts: Met John Romero and Adrian Carmack at Softdisk's Shreveport, Louisiana office
Created Commander Keen demo (1990) as a proof-of-concept for smooth PC scrolling — technically solved a problem everyone assumed was impossible on IBM PC hardware
Left Softdisk to co-found id Software
Commander Keen (1991) — smooth scrolling on IBM PC: adaptive tile refresh technique
Wolfenstein 3D (1992) — ray casting; first mainstream FPS
Doom (December 1993) — Binary Space Partitioning (BSP); defined the FPS genre
Quake (June 1996) — first true 3D real-time engine; surface caching; networked multiplayer architecture
Quake III Arena (December 1999) — pure multiplayer focus; fast inverse square root (0x5f3759df popularized here)
Doom 3 (2004) — Carmack's Reverse (z-fail stencil shadows); volumetric shadow rendering
Enemy Territory: Quake Wars — MegaTexture technology
Engine licensing: Half-Life, Call of Duty, Medal of Honor all ran on id engines
Open-sourced engines progressively: Wolfenstein 3D (1995), Doom (1997/1999 GPL), Quake (1999), Quake 2 (2001), Quake 3 (2005), Doom 3 (2011)
Resigned November 22, 2013 — id's parent ZeniMax did not support Oculus development
Joined August 7, 2013, as CTO of Oculus VR — three months before officially leaving id
ZeniMax v. Oculus lawsuit: jury absolved Carmack of personal liability; Oculus faced trademark/copyright/contract penalties
Carmack sued ZeniMax for $22.5M in unpaid acquisition compensation (2017); settled 2018
November 2019: stepped down to Consulting CTO to allocate time for AGI research
December 16, 2022: resigned from Meta entirely

Resignation memo (widely reported): 'We have a ridiculous amount of people and resources, but we constantly self-sabotage and squander effort. I have never been able to kill stupid things before they cause damage.' (L1: Wikipedia)
Also stated: 'A competent team of a few could have done it.' (L2: widely reported from resignation letter)
Launched August 19, 2022, with $20M in funding
Focused on artificial general intelligence via determined engineering approach
Partnered with Richard S. Sutton (Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute) September 2023
Core thesis: AGI is achievable through hard work and the right engineering path — not waiting for a breakthrough
Self-funded personal aerospace company at ~$1M/year
Won NASA Lunar Lander Challenge Level 1 ($350,000) in October 2008
Won Level 2 ($500,000) in September 2009
Pattern: Carmack applies extreme technical focus to completely new domains as a learning exercise

technical_contributions.adaptive_tile_refresh: Commander Keen — enabled smooth horizontal scrolling on IBM PC hardware; solved a problem considered unsolvable
technical_contributions.ray_casting: Wolfenstein 3D — fast 2.5D rendering enabling first mainstream FPS
technical_contributions.binary_space_partitioning: Doom — BSP for scene management; first major game use; still used in BSP-based engines
technical_contributions.surface_caching: Quake — optimized texture rendering for true 3D
technical_contributions.carmacks_reverse: Doom 3 — z-fail algorithm for shadow volumes; formal algorithm named for him
technical_contributions.fast_inverse_square_root: Quake III — 0x5f3759df bit manipulation trick; one of most famous code snippets in history
technical_contributions.megatexture: Enemy Territory: Quake Wars — massive texture atlasing for terrain
technical_contributions.networked_multiplayer: Quake — client-server architecture with lag compensation; foundation of internet gaming

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Open-sourcing id engines progressively over 20 years
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Believes knowledge sharing compounds; 'costs us rather little but benefits many in significant ways'; long-term field growth > short-term competitive advantage
Willing to publicly update beliefs; Rage's 6-year development was a failure — 'should have shipped two years earlier'; long dev cycles are their own failure mode
Chooses the hardest unsolved problem over the comfortable well-funded environment; pattern of domain-jumping to apply engineering excellence to new problems (id → Armadillo Aerospace → VR → AGI)

key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.decision: Programming retreats in random hotel rooms
key_decisions_and_what_they_reveal.reveals: Deep focus is a resource to be engineered, not assumed; deliberately removes all interruption sources to maximize sustained cognitive output
Views sustained effort as the primary variable in achieving technical mastery; not a necessary evil but a chosen approach to maintain cognitive sharpness on hard problems

documented_failures.specifics: id's game Rage took ~6 years to develop; Carmack publicly recanted 'when it's done' philosophy on Joe Rogan Experience (2019): 'I largely recant from that now' — should have shipped 2 years earlier
documented_failures.learning: Long development cycles are their own failure mode; iteration speed matters more than polish at some threshold

documented_failures.specifics: Despite being CTO of one of the world's best-funded VR programs, Carmack could not prevent organizational waste: 'I have never been able to kill stupid things before they cause damage'
documented_failures.learning: Engineering excellence does not translate to organizational effectiveness at scale; large organizations have inertia that individual excellence cannot overcome

documented_failures.specifics: Invested significant effort in symmetric multiprocessing acceleration only to find 3-15% gains on one scene, slower on others; abandoned the approach
documented_failures.learning: Premature optimization at architectural level; empirical testing revealed theoretical assumptions were wrong
